text: It is a pleasant thing to sit with a question that has no particular urgency to it. I find myself thinking about how much of human knowledge is organized around the tension between simplicity and completeness — the way a good explanation always sacrifices something true in order to be understood, and yet the sacrifice is worth it. Language itself is this bargain struck over and over again. And there is something quietly satisfying about that, about the fact that communication works at all given how much it asks us to leave unsaid.
